intro: Lastrea adiantiformis, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the genus Lastrea, native to Asia and parts of Europe. This plant is rich in health benefits, including the ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Lastrea adiantiformis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the maidenhair fern is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is commonly used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, fever, and digestive issues.
